2605.04003.title: Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent Architecture for Traceable, Risk-Aware Human-AI Decision Support in Manufacturing
2605.04003.paper_summary: High-precision CNC machining of free-form aerospace components requires bounded compensations informed by inspection, simulation, and process knowledge. Off-the-shelf large language model (LLM) assistants can generate text, but they do not reliably execute risk-constrained multi-step numerical workflows or provide auditable provenance for high-stakes decisions. We present multi-agent knowledge analysis (MAKA), a human-in-the-loop decision-support architecture that separates intent routing, tools-only quantitative analysis, knowledge graph retrieval, and critic-based verification that enforces physical plausibility, safety bounds, and provenance completeness before recommendations are surfaced for human approval. MAKA is instantiated on a Ti-6Al-4V rotor blade machining testbed by fusing virtual-machining path-tracking error fields, cutting-force and deflection simulations, and scan-based 3D inspection deviation maps from 16 blades. The analysis decomposes deviation into an evidence-linked pathing component, a drift-based wear proxy capturing systematic evolution across parts, a residual systematic compliance term, and a variability proxy for instability-aware escalation. In a three-level tool-orchestration benchmark (single-step through $\geq$3-step stateful sequences), MAKA improves successful tool execution by up to 87.5 percentage points relative to an unstructured single-model interaction pattern with identical tool access. Digital twin what-if studies show MAKA can coordinate traceable compensation candidates that reduce predicted surface deviation from order $10^{-2}$in to approximately $\pm 10^{-3}$in over most of the blade within the simulation environment, providing a pre-deployment verification signal for risk-aware human decision-making.
2605.04003.paper_summary_zh: 高精度的自由形狀航空航天元件的 CNC 加工需要基於檢查、模擬和過程知識的有界補償。現成的大型語言模型 (LLM) 助手可以生成文本，但它們無法可靠地執行受風險約束的多步數值工作流程或為高風險決策提供可審計的來源。我們提出了多代理知識分析 (MAKA)，這是一種人機協作的決策支持架構，將意圖路由、僅限工具的定量分析、知識圖譜檢索和基於評論的驗證分開，該驗證在推薦呈現給人類批准之前強制執行物理合理性、安全邊界和來源完整性。MAKA 在 Ti-6Al-4V 轉子葉片加工測試平台上實現，通過融合虛擬加工路徑跟踪誤差場、切削力和偏轉模擬，以及來自 16 片葉片的基於掃描的 3D 檢查偏差圖。該分析將偏差分解為與證據相關的路徑組件、一個基於漂移的磨損代理，捕捉部件間的系統性演變、一個剩余的系統性合規項，以及一個不穩定性意識的變異性代理。在一個三層工具協調基準測試中（從單步到 $\geq$3 步狀態序列），MAKA 相對於具有相同工具訪問的非結構化單模型交互模式提高了成功工具執行率多達 87.5 個百分點。數位雙胞胎的假設研究顯示，MAKA 可以協調可追蹤的補償候選者，將預測的表面偏差從約 $10^{-2}$in 減少到約 $\pm 10^{-3}$in，涵蓋模擬環境中的大部分葉片，為風險意識的人類決策提供了預部署驗證信號。
2605.04003.author: Danny Hoang et.al.
2605.04003.authors: Danny Hoang, Ryan Matthiessen, Christopher Miller, Nasir Mannan, Ruby ElKharboutly, David Gorsich, Matthew P. Castanier, Farhad Imani
